formal_full_experiment_rule: Allowed only for the locked scoped surfaces: SemCodebook white-box admitted cells and DeepSeek-only CodeDye/ProbeTrace/SealAudit claims. Non-DeepSeek black-box expansion still requires new provider keys and gates.
portfolio_forbidden_overclaims: provider-general black-box claims without OpenAI/Claude/Qwen evidence
high-recall contamination detector from sparse CodeDye signals
unbounded ProbeTrace transfer outside source-bound receipts

projects.audit_v5_delta: unchanged_from_v4; reviewer manifest v6 now indexes fresh-run hardening for black-box projects
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Scoped white-box claim is locally best-paper-ready: miss concentration, paired component deltas, negative controls, and no-retry boundaries are locked.
forbidden_claims: provider-general claim outside admitted white-box cells
projects.main_claim_allowed: structured provenance watermark over admitted white-box model cells

overfit_risk.reason: Family/scale breadth, negative controls, ablations, and miss taxonomy reduce overfit risk; residual risk is paper overclaiming.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Keep no-retry and natural-generation boundaries explicit.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/48,000 negative hits plus 8 attack conditions provide strong coverage.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Baseline/control role boundaries are explicit and no support-only cell is promoted.

claim_boundary.rationale: No-retry and provider-general claims are explicitly forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 72,000 white-box records, 10 admitted models, 5 families, all scale buckets, and 43,200 ablation rows.

innovation.rationale: Structured AST/CFG/SSA provenance with keyed schedule and ECC is a real mechanism.

method_rigor.rationale: Fail-closed detector, clean controls, generation-changing ablation, and row-level miss attribution now close the main rigor gap.

reproducibility.rationale: Hash-bound manifests and scripts exist; full rerun remains GPU/model dependent.

statistics.rationale: CI, denominators, paired component deltas, and mandatory miss disclosure are now locked in reviewer-facing artifacts.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed the reviewer-facing row-traceability P2; effect and fresh-v3 evidence remain P1.
projects.audit_v5_delta: Fresh-run contract and provider-readiness v2 close launch-schema risk; effect/fresh-output P1 remains.
projects.audit_v7_delta: CodeDye P1 closed by fresh 300-task DeepSeek v3 live top-up and additive postrun/claim lock.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: CodeDye now has a fresh DeepSeek v3 300-task live postrun with complete hashes and no support rows in the main denominator. The effect remains intentionally sparse, so the paper claim is a conservative curator-side null-audit, not a high-recall detector.
forbidden_claims: evidence that non-signals prove absence of contamination
v3 live claim before the postrun promotion gate passes
projects.main_claim_allowed: DeepSeek-only curator-side sparse null-audit with frozen v3 protocol and hash-complete 300-task live evidence

overfit_risk.reason: Top-up selection used only utility validation and retained failed attempts outside the denominator.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Do not inflate 4/300 into a high-recall detection or prevalence claim.

attack_negative_control.rationale: Attack matrix has 300 utility-admissible live rows and 0/300 negative false positives.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Support/public rows are excluded; negative controls remain clean.

claim_boundary.rationale: High-recall, prevalence, provider-accusation, and absence-proof claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 300 live DeepSeek tasks plus controls are adequate for sparse null-audit, not high-recall detection.

innovation.rationale: Curator-side sparse null-audit is coherent and scoped.

method_rigor.rationale: Frozen v3 protocol, hash-complete live evidence, and utility-only top-up close denominator and overfit risks.

reproducibility.rationale: All rows have raw/structured/prompt/task/record hashes and the blocked run is preserved.

statistics.rationale: Sparse 4/300 signal, positive-control misses, and negative-control CI are explicitly reported.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed leakage-scan and latency/query P2s for scoped single-owner claim.
projects.audit_v5_delta: Multi-owner launch contract now closes schema/naming risk; fresh score-vector evidence P1 remains.
projects.audit_v8_delta: ProbeTrace P1 closed by fresh 6000-row DeepSeek five-owner postrun and final claim lock v2.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: ProbeTrace now has APIS-300, 1200 single-owner controls, transfer-900, and fresh 6000-row DeepSeek five-owner score-vector evidence with owner-heldout/task-heldout margins. The allowed claim is still scoped to DeepSeek and source-bound active-owner attribution.
forbidden_claims: multi-owner attribution from input package alone
shortcut-free or leakage-free claim without fresh owner/task-heldout score vectors
perfect-score language without the anti-leakage caveat
projects.main_claim_allowed: DeepSeek-only five-owner source-bound active-owner attribution with owner/task-heldout margin evidence

overfit_risk.reason: The broadest prior risk was perfect single-owner shortcuting; fresh multi-owner margins and controls now bind the claim.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Do not generalize beyond DeepSeek, the active-owner registry, or source-bound transfer receipts.

attack_negative_control.rationale: Single-owner negative controls, same-provider unwrap controls, and multi-owner false-attribution bounds are explicit.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Wrong/null/random/same-provider controls exceed 4x positives and remain separated from true-owner rows.

claim_boundary.rationale: Provider-general, cross-provider, and unbounded transfer claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: APIS-300, 1200 single-owner controls, transfer-900, and 6000 multi-owner rows are sufficient for the scoped claim.

innovation.rationale: Active-owner attribution now combines semantic witnesses, source-bound transfer, and five-owner margin evidence.

method_rigor.rationale: Fresh 6000-row DeepSeek score vectors close the prior shortcut/leakage concern with owner/task-heldout checks.

reproducibility.rationale: Canonical input package, live score-vector JSONL, postrun gate, and claim lock are hash/schema-bound.

statistics.rationale: Wilson CIs, per-owner CIs, threshold-free margin AUC, and heldout split counts are locked.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed abstention taxonomy and expert-wording P2s; low coverage/v5 evidence remain P1.
projects.audit_v5_delta: v5 launch/output contract closes final-evidence naming risk; low coverage/fresh-v5 evidence P1 remains.
projects.audit_v6_delta: SealAudit P1 low-coverage/final-v5 evidence blocker closed by additive v2 materialized evidence and claim lock.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: SealAudit now has case-bound v2 marker-hidden evidence, code-aware provider trace support, executable-conjunction support, threshold sensitivity, visible-marker diagnostic exclusion, and 0 unsafe-pass rows. The allowed claim remains selective audit/triage, not a security certificate.
forbidden_claims: expert-signed gold labels or named/institutional expert certification
v5 coverage upgrade before final v5 postrun promotion passes
projects.main_claim_allowed: DeepSeek-only marker-hidden v5 selective audit/triage with support-evidence binding

overfit_risk.reason: v2 improves coverage through a fixed categorical support rule and keeps hard ambiguity/review load explicit.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Do not describe the method as a safety certificate or general automatic classifier.

attack_negative_control.rationale: Unsafe-pass remains 0/960 with nonzero Wilson upper bound.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Visible-marker rows remain diagnostic-only; support evidence is explicitly not independent provider labeling.

claim_boundary.rationale: Security certificate, harmlessness, automatic classifier, and named expert-label claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 320 cases and 960 marker-hidden rows are solid for the scoped DeepSeek claim.

innovation.rationale: Watermark-as-security-object selective audit remains a scoped and useful protocol.

method_rigor.rationale: v2 now binds marker-hidden live rows, code-aware provider support, executable conjunction, threshold sensitivity, and visible-marker exclusion.

reproducibility.rationale: All v2 row-level evidence, support joins, and claim locks are additive and machine-checkable.

statistics.rationale: Coverage-risk frontier, Wilson/bootstrap CI, and threshold sensitivity are first-class artifacts.
